summary:
The most appropriate method for submandibular calculus removal depends on size and location; smaller, accessible calculi may respond well to minimally invasive techniques like sialendoscopy or duct slitting, while larger or obstructive lesions require transoral surgical intervention involving direct duct incision and extraction. In all scenarios, complete glandectomy is only indicated when there are persistent recurrences despite other treatments.
